source_findings.raw_text: Benchmark promotion requires human approval before a fixture becomes part of replayable suites.
AI-generated reflections or rule proposals may never directly modify production rules.
Human approval is mandatory for rule acceptance, benchmark promotion, and override of critical regressions.
Convert approved representative failures into versioned benchmark fixtures and record failure-to-fixture lineage.
Keep AI-generated rule or prompt proposals in proposal logs only until human approval occurs.
Use rule-based checks in production runtime; confine LLM-as-judge and Reflexion-style loops to sandbox analysis unless explicitly frozen and approved.
AI-on-AI degradation where evaluator or reflection bias shapes production rules indirectly.
Run outputs against frozen style fingerprints and confirm style drift is flagged while approved intentional shifts remain allowed only through governance.

proposals.description: Propose a governance rule set for human approval boundaries.
proposals.rationale: The system-design and evaluation findings both require explicit human-gated promotion and replay-based release discipline.
proposals.expected_impact: Protects the source of truth from ungated evaluator drift, AI-on-AI feedback loops, and silent regression.
risks: process fatigue
overly rigid gates slowing useful evolution
criteria ambiguity if not specified concretely later

source_findings.raw_text: Only validated failures may be promoted into benchmark fixtures.
Benchmark promotion requires human approval before a fixture becomes part of replayable suites.
Evaluator prompts, thresholds, and model versions must be frozen for any baseline used in promotion decisions.
System changes must pass sandbox evaluation before shadow evaluation.
No change may be promoted to production without benchmark replay against the relevant fixture set.
Human approval is mandatory for rule acceptance, benchmark promotion, and override of critical regressions.
Reflexion-style self-reflection may run only in sandboxed improvement loops, never as a live production writer or updater.
Promotion decisions must reject Pareto-unsafe changes that improve one dimension while dropping another below minimum thresholds.
External dependency changes, including evaluator or model-version changes, require fresh benchmark replay before promotion.
Run multi-metric regression suites on frozen fixtures before any architecture, prompt, rule, or model change is promoted.
Generate `system_change_proposal` artifacts for every candidate update and route them through sandbox then shadow evaluation.
Use rule-based checks in production runtime; confine LLM-as-judge and Reflexion-style loops to sandbox analysis unless explicitly frozen and approved.
Failure-to-fixture bloat caused by uncontrolled promotion and duplicate fixtures.
Stable-but-wrong promotion where deterministic outputs still fail core quality or correctness goals.
Reflection-style tone contamination where sandbox reflections bias prose toward explanatory blandness.
Submit duplicate failure cases to fixture-promotion flow and verify deterministic deduplication prevents benchmark bloat.

source_findings.raw_text: All core evaluation paths MUST be deterministic for fixed inputs and configuration in the deterministic writing architecture.
Configure evaluators (LLM-judges, discriminators, metrics) to run with greedy decoding and fixed seeds to ensure deterministic behavior.
For any change to prompts, rubrics, or fixtures, record a new version identifier and run prompt sensitivity and stability checks.
Apply template echo detector to sample batches for each major prompt or fine-tune version, adjusting thresholds using benchmark fixtures.
All production evaluation paths must be deterministic for fixed inputs, versions, and configuration.
Failure taxonomy changes must be versioned and may not silently rewrite historical labels.
Evaluator prompts, thresholds, and model versions must be frozen for any baseline used in promotion decisions.
Systemic-versus-one-off failure classification must use deterministic clustering or deterministic feature analytics in primary workflows.
Style fingerprints must be versioned and frozen between releases unless changed through governance.
External dependency changes, including evaluator or model-version changes, require fresh benchmark replay before promotion.
Write every failure event to `failure_log.jsonl` with fixture id, pipeline stage, version metadata, evaluator outputs, and resolution state.
Maintain `failure_taxonomy.json` as a controlled vocabulary with explicit versioning.
Cluster failure-ledger entries periodically to identify systemic spikes by fixture, genre, style, failure type, and model version.
Convert approved representative failures into versioned benchmark fixtures and record failure-to-fixture lineage.
Freeze evaluator prompts, thresholds, and configs per release so score comparisons remain historically meaningful.
Load continuity state snapshots before generation or refinement and emit deterministic `state_diff` artifacts after each segment.

source_findings.raw_text: Evaluator prompts, thresholds, and model versions must be frozen for any baseline used in promotion decisions.
Style fingerprints must be versioned and frozen between releases unless changed through governance.
Production runtime style enforcement must be rule-based or use frozen numeric thresholds from hybrid evaluators.
State transitions must be logged as explicit diffs and may not retroactively rewrite canonical state without governance.
